name: Untether AI
description: Toronto-based Untether AI raised $150M+ to build AI chips but missed the generative AI wave, couldn't compete with Nvidia, and filed for bankruptcy in 2025.
story: Founded around 2018 in Toronto, Untether AI raised over $150 million to develop energy-efficient AI inference chips. The company bet on specialized hardware for AI workloads at the edge, but missed the generative AI wave that rewarded GPU-based training infrastructure. Pivoting too late toward hardware for large language models, Untether couldn't compete with Nvidia's dominant ecosystem. Trump-era tariff uncertainty further complicated its hardware supply chain and spooked potential investors. In 2025, the company filed for bankruptcy with $128 million in liabilities. AMD acqui-hired the engineering team but notably did not acquire Untether's technology. The founders moved on to start a new company called Hepzibah AI — a fresh attempt in a market that had already passed Untether by.

sources.title: Untether AI shuts down, team joins AMD
